summary: Completed full backend testing of Startup Roast API (auth, roast generation with Gemini 3 Flash, Razorpay payment flow, leaderboard, my roasts). 24/24 tests passing after minor test fix (email case normalization). All critical flows verified end-to-end including free-roast→402→payment→paid-roast→balance-decrement.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~400 lines - acceptable but near threshold. Consider splitting routes (auth, roast, payment) into separate routers if it grows further.

payment/verify does not check current authenticated user == order owner BEFORE signature verification. Not a security issue (HMAC check blocks forging), but ordering could leak timing info. Low priority.
ROAST_PRICE_PAISE hardcoded (4900). Consider moving to .env for flexibility.

generate_roast_ai: when balance==0 and free not used, the entitlement is consumed AFTER AI call succeeds - good. However if AI call fails with 502, user doesn't lose entitlement - correct behavior, verified in code path.
CORS allows * by default when CORS_ORIGINS not set - acceptable for dev but should be locked down in prod.
test_credentials: Used existing roast1@test.com/password123 + dynamically registered TEST_roast_<uuid>@test.com users
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates a fresh TEST_roast_<uuid>@test.com user (not cleaned up - data harmless since idempotent UUID-based emails)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend 100% green. Tests cover: register/login/me, JWT auth (401 missing/invalid), free-roast flow, 402 when balance=0, Razorpay order creation (real Razorpay API call - test mode), HMAC signature verify (both valid via manual hmac-sha256 hexdigest and invalid), paid_roasts_balance increment/decrement, leaderboard sort asc by score, my-roasts scoping to user, single roast GET, idea length validation (<15 and >4000). Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_roast_backend.py -v
rca of the issue: N/A - all tests pass. Minor assertion bug in my test (expected original-case email, but API correctly lowercases) was fixed in test.
